findings.description: Comprehensive CI/CD infrastructure with 18 specialized GitHub Actions workflows covering testing, linting, coverage, performance, security, and documentation.
findings.evidence: Workflows identified in .github/workflows/: tests.yml, linters.yml, coverage_tests.yml, coverage_comment.yml, selenium.yml, postgis.yml, check-migrations.yml, check_commit_messages.yml, check_pr_quality.yml, benchmark.yml, docs.yml, screenshots.yml, schedule_tests.yml, schedules.yml, python_matrix.yml, new_contributor_pr.yml, labels.yml, and zizmor.yml security scanning. Latest commit (SHA: 61a62be313e395ce1265132bfc99f51476fb3c95) from April 22, 2026 shows active continuous integration.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current CI/CD practices. Consider documenting the workflow architecture for new contributors.

findings.description: Exceptional code quality enforcement through automated tooling and pre-commit hooks. Zero TODO/FIXME markers found in codebase.
findings.evidence: Configuration files present: .flake8 (297 bytes), .pre-commit-config.yaml (797 bytes), pyproject.toml with [tool.black] and [tool.isort] sections configured. Code search for 'TODO FIXME' returned 0 results (total_count: 0, incomplete_results: false). .editorconfig (697 bytes) ensures consistent coding standards.
findings.recommendation: Continue enforcing quality standards through pre-commit hooks. Current approach is exemplary.

findings.description: Highly efficient PR management with rapid merge cycles for fixes and thorough review for features. Strong quality gates prevent low-quality submissions.
findings.evidence: Recent merged PRs: #21152 merged in ~5.5 hours (created Apr 22 16:01, merged 21:28). Open PRs sample shows 9/10 are recent (≤4 days old): #21154, #21153, #21150 (all Apr 22), #21148 (Apr 21), #21145, #21144 (Apr 20). Rejected PRs #21151 and #21149 closed within seconds due to quality gate failures. PR template enforces Trac ticket linking, AI disclosure, comprehensive checklist.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current PR review standards. No stale PR backlog observed.

findings.description: Professional repository organization following industry best practices with clear separation of source, tests, documentation, and tooling.
findings.evidence: Root directory contains 28 well-organized items including: django/ (source), tests/ (test suite), docs/ (documentation), js_tests/ (JavaScript tests), scripts/ (utilities), .github/ (CI/CD). Documentation files present: README.rst (2,017 bytes), CONTRIBUTING.rst (1,115 bytes), AUTHORS (45,163 bytes contributor history). Modern Python packaging with pyproject.toml (2,167 bytes), no legacy setup.py.
findings.recommendation: Structure is exemplary. No changes needed.

findings.description: Comprehensive multi-layered testing strategy covering unit, integration, browser, and database-specific tests with coverage tracking.
findings.evidence: Dedicated test workflows: tests.yml, selenium.yml (browser testing), postgis.yml (database-specific), coverage_tests.yml + coverage_comment.yml (coverage tracking), benchmark.yml (performance regression). Separate tests/ directory and js_tests/ directory for frontend. tox.ini (2,195 bytes) for multi-environment testing.
findings.recommendation: Testing infrastructure is world-class. Continue current practices.

findings.description: Modern dependency management using PEP 621 compliant pyproject.toml with minimal runtime dependencies and clear optional dependency groups.
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml specifies: requires-python >= 3.12, build-system requires setuptools>=77.0.3, core dependencies limited to asgiref>=3.9.1, sqlparse>=0.5.0, tzdata (Windows only). Optional dependencies properly grouped: argon2-cffi>=23.1.0, bcrypt>=4.1.1. JavaScript dependencies managed via package.json (367 bytes).
findings.recommendation: Dependency management follows best practices. No lock file needed for library/framework projects.

findings.description: Highly active development with multiple commits merged daily and consistent contributor engagement.
findings.evidence: Last 10 commits show activity from April 18-22, 2026. Recent commits include: Fixed #37057 (Apr 22 by charettes, committed by jacobtylerwalls), Fixed #35870 (Apr 22 by annalauraw), Fixed #36991 (Apr 22 by DineshThumma9), Fixed #15759 (Apr 22), and 6 more. Multiple contributors active including charettes, jacobtylerwalls, annalauraw, DineshThumma9, cliffordgama, felixxm, varunkasyap.
findings.recommendation: Development velocity is excellent. Contributor diversity indicates healthy community.

findings.description: Comprehensive documentation infrastructure with ReadTheDocs integration, dedicated docs workflow, and thorough contributor guidelines.
findings.evidence: Files present: .readthedocs.yml (410 bytes), docs.yml workflow, CONTRIBUTING.rst (1,115 bytes), README.rst (2,017 bytes). Recent documentation PRs: #21153 (documentation clarification), #21150 (documented admin decorator parameters), #21138 (improved docs home page organization).
findings.recommendation: Documentation practices are exemplary. Continue maintaining comprehensive docs for all features.

findings.description: Rigorous code review process with mandatory checklists, Trac ticket integration, and AI assistance disclosure requirements.
findings.evidence: PR template enforces: Trac ticket linking, AI assistance disclosure (required field), contribution guidelines compliance, security vulnerability check, commit message format verification, test coverage requirements. Examples: #21154 disclosed Claude Opus 4.7 usage, #21150 disclosed ChatGPT for grammar review, #21148 disclosed Gemini assistance.
findings.recommendation: Code review process is thorough and transparent. AI disclosure requirement is forward-thinking.

findings.description: Dedicated performance regression detection through automated benchmark workflow.
findings.evidence: benchmark.yml workflow (1,529 bytes) present in .github/workflows/. PR #21145 labeled with 'benchmark' tag indicating performance-critical changes are tracked.
findings.recommendation: Performance monitoring is proactive. Maintain benchmark coverage for critical code paths.

findings.description: Security policy is present and references comprehensive external documentation at djangoproject.com/security/. The repository contains a SECURITY.md file that delegates to Django's official security program.
findings.evidence: File: .github/SECURITY.md (SHA: 58d191de54b8b93de9c67ebbc1fa0b5d183b2921) contains 'Please see https://www.djangoproject.com/security/'. Django maintains a well-established responsible disclosure program with documented security issue reporting procedures.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Security policy is properly documented and follows Django's established security governance model.

findings.description: No dedicated Python security static analysis tools (CodeQL, Bandit, Semgrep) are configured in CI/CD pipeline. Only general code linters (flake8, black, isort) are present.
findings.evidence: Workflow file .github/workflows/linters.yml (SHA: ccee1adde022eccc84a9b95bce82294161d4212c) contains flake8, isort, black, zizmor, and biome but no security-specific Python SAST tools. Search query 'repo:django/django codeql path:.github/workflows' returned 0 results.
findings.recommendation: Add Bandit or Semgrep to the CI pipeline to detect security vulnerabilities such as SQL injection patterns, hardcoded secrets, unsafe deserialization, and other Python-specific security issues. Consider enabling GitHub CodeQL for comprehensive security analysis.

findings.description: No automated dependency vulnerability scanning tool (Dependabot, Renovate) is configured. The repository lacks automated monitoring for vulnerable dependencies.
findings.evidence: File .github/dependabot.yml does not exist (file not found error returned). No Renovate configuration detected in repository tree. Review of 19 workflow files found no dependency scanning workflows.
findings.recommendation: Enable GitHub Dependabot by creating .github/dependabot.yml to automatically detect and alert on vulnerable dependencies. At minimum, configure security-only updates to catch CVEs in dependencies.

findings.description: GitHub Actions use mixed pinning strategies - some actions are SHA-pinned (best practice) while others use mutable tag references, creating supply chain attack risk.
findings.evidence: In .github/workflows/linters.yml: SHA-pinned actions include 'liskin/gh-problem-matcher-wrap@e7b7beaaafa52524748b31a381160759d68d61fb' and 'zizmorcore/zizmor-action@e639db99335bc9038abc0e066dfcd72e23d26fb4'. Tag-only pinned actions include 'actions/checkout@v6', 'actions/setup-python@v6', 'psf/black@stable'.
findings.recommendation: Migrate all GitHub Actions to SHA-256 commit pinning to prevent supply chain attacks via compromised action tags. Replace version tags with full commit SHAs and add comments with version numbers for maintainability.

findings.description: Limited use of potentially dangerous shell execution patterns. Only 2 instances of shell=True found in controlled contexts outside production runtime code.
findings.evidence: Search for 'shell=True' in Python code returned 2 results: django/utils/version.py (version detection utility) and scripts/manage_translations.py (build/development script, not production runtime). No matches found for common dangerous patterns in production code paths.
findings.recommendation: Continue current practices of limiting shell=True usage to build tooling and development scripts. Ensure any new shell execution uses list-based arguments (shell=False) or proper input sanitization.

findings.description: GitHub Actions workflow security is actively monitored using zizmor security linter, which scans for workflow vulnerabilities and misconfigurations.
findings.evidence: Workflow .github/workflows/linters.yml includes 'zizmorcore/zizmor-action@e639db99335bc9038abc0e066dfcd72e23d26fb4' which runs on all pull requests and main branch pushes with 'advanced-security: false' and 'annotations: true' configured.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Continue using zizmor for workflow security scanning. Consider enabling advanced-security features if GitHub Advanced Security license is available.

findings.description: GitHub Advanced Security features (secret scanning alerts, code scanning alerts, Dependabot alerts) cannot be verified as they require admin permissions on the repository.
findings.evidence: Public repository status prevents verification of security alerts via API (would return 403 Forbidden). These features may be enabled but are not publicly observable. No public code scanning results or security advisories are visible in the assessed workflow configurations.
findings.recommendation: If repository admins have not already done so, enable GitHub Advanced Security features including secret scanning, code scanning with CodeQL, and Dependabot security updates through repository settings.

findings.description: Django maintains mature security governance with dedicated security team, established disclosure process, and public security advisory archive. This is appropriate for a security-critical web framework.
findings.evidence: SECURITY.md references https://www.djangoproject.com/security/ which documents Django's security policy, security team, reporting procedures, supported versions, and security issue archive. Django has a well-established track record of responsible vulnerability disclosure.
findings.recommendation: Continue current security governance practices. Ensure integration between GitHub repository security features and the Django security team's existing processes.

findings.description: Primary LICENSE file present with complete BSD 3-Clause license text
findings.evidence: File: LICENSE (SHA: 5f4f225dd282aa7e4361ec3c2750bbbaaed8ab1f). Content: 'Copyright (c) Django Software Foundation and individual contributors. All rights reserved. Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions are met...' Full BSD 3-Clause license text with proper copyright attribution.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining the LICENSE file with current copyright holders and license terms.

findings.description: Additional LICENSE.python file documents third-party code attribution
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows LICENSE.python file (14,256 bytes, SHA: 2fc28e876fa1fc8c536f46b81789e2bb51b323c0) present at root level, documenting Python Software Foundation license for incorporated code.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining LICENSE.python to properly attribute incorporated PSF code.

findings.description: Comprehensive AUTHORS file present with extensive contributor attribution
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows AUTHORS file (45,163 bytes, SHA: 07c87068a9d3b46491391818053839dcd5ffcf1e) present at root level, providing comprehensive contributor attribution.
findings.recommendation: Continue updating AUTHORS file as new contributors join the project.

findings.description: License properly declared in pyproject.toml using SPDX identifier
findings.evidence: File: pyproject.toml (SHA: c64cd28dab46fc6f1a24ef3e963cd6bd54186c93). Content: 'license = "BSD-3-Clause"' and 'license-files = ["LICENSE", "LICENSE.python", "AUTHORS"]' in [project] section.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current SPDX identifier usage and PEP 639 compliance in project metadata.

findings.description: PyPI classifier includes license information for package discoverability
findings.evidence: File: pyproject.toml. Content: classifiers array includes 'License :: OSI Approved :: BSD License' classifier for PyPI metadata.
findings.recommendation: Keep PyPI license classifier in sync with declared SPDX license identifier.

findings.description: Most source files lack license headers or SPDX identifiers
findings.evidence: Search query 'repo:django/django "Copyright (c) Django Software Foundation" language:Python' returned 0 matches. File django/__init__.py (SHA: 4402600c576fab7d8e88389799558aa963369b2d) contains no license header. Only 5 files found with copyright notices via search 'repo:django/django path:django/ copyright language:Python'.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding minimal SPDX headers (e.g., '# SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause') to improve machine-readability for automated license scanning tools, though not required for BSD licenses.

findings.description: All declared dependencies use permissive licenses compatible with BSD 3-Clause
findings.evidence: File: pyproject.toml. Dependencies declared: 'asgiref>=3.9.1' (BSD), 'sqlparse>=0.5.0' (BSD), 'tzdata; sys_platform == "win32"' (Apache 2.0). Optional dependencies: 'argon2-cffi>=23.1.0' (MIT), 'bcrypt>=4.1.1' (Apache 2.0). All are permissive licenses compatible with BSD 3-Clause.
findings.recommendation: Implement automated license scanning in CI/CD pipeline to monitor for license changes in dependencies during updates.

findings.description: BSD 3-Clause is a permissive, OSI-approved license allowing broad usage
findings.evidence: LICENSE file contains full BSD 3-Clause license text: '1. Redistributions of source code must retain the above copyright notice... 2. Redistributions in binary form must reproduce the above copyright notice... 3. Neither the name of Django nor the names of its contributors may be used to endorse or promote products...'
findings.recommendation: No action needed. The BSD 3-Clause license provides clear terms for use, modification, and distribution.

findings.description: Project demonstrates excellent license clarity with multiple attribution files and consistent metadata
findings.evidence: Repository root contains: LICENSE (BSD 3-Clause), LICENSE.python (PSF attribution), AUTHORS (comprehensive contributors). Metadata in pyproject.toml declares 'license = "BSD-3-Clause"' with 'license-files = ["LICENSE", "LICENSE.python", "AUTHORS"]'. All elements are properly cross-referenced.
findings.recommendation: Continue current practices. Django serves as an exemplary model for open-source license compliance.

findings.description: GitHub Issues are intentionally disabled in favor of Trac-based issue tracking. Django uses an external Trac instance (https://code.djangoproject.com) for bug tracking and feature requests, which is a deliberate architectural choice for this mature project.
findings.evidence: list_issues returned totalCount=0 for both OPEN and CLOSED states. CONTRIBUTING.rst states: 'Warning: non-trivial pull requests (anything more than fixing a typo) without Trac tickets will be closed! Please file a ticket to suggest changes.' and 'Django uses Trac to keep track of bugs, feature requests, and associated patches because GitHub doesn't provide adequate tooling for its community.'
findings.recommendation: Continue documenting the Trac workflow clearly. Consider adding a QUICKSTART.md specifically for first-time contributors to navigate the Trac system, as this may be unfamiliar to GitHub-native developers.

findings.description: No issue templates found in .github directory, though this is consistent with the Trac-based workflow where issue creation happens outside GitHub.
findings.evidence: search_code query for 'repo:django/django path:.github ISSUE_TEMPLATE' returned total_count=0. The .github directory contains CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, SECURITY.md, pull_request_template.md, FUNDING.yml, and copilot-instructions.md, but no ISSUE_TEMPLATE directory.
findings.recommendation: Current approach is appropriate given the Trac-based workflow. No action needed.

findings.description: Healthy contributor diversity with low bus factor risk. Recent commits show multiple active maintainers and diverse contributors including new participants.
findings.evidence: list_commits (last 10 commits, April 2026) shows: jacobtylerwalls (6 commits as committer), sarahboyce (1 commit as committer), felixxm (1 commit), and 6 different contributors including DineshThumma9 (new contributor, ID 160909147), charettes, annalauraw, aadeina, cliffordgama, and varunkasyap. AUTHORS file is 45,163 bytes indicating extensive historical contributor tracking.
findings.recommendation: Continue fostering diverse contributions. The current model of having multiple core maintainers who review and commit others' work is working excellently.

findings.description: Active use of co-authorship attribution demonstrates collaborative development culture and proper credit distribution.
findings.evidence: Recent commits show co-authorship patterns: commit 63c56cda 'Co-Authored-By: Marijke Luttekes <mail@marijkeluttekes.dev>', commit e1bdebc8 'Co-authored-by: Peter van der Does <peter@oneilinteractive.com> Co-authored-by: Jacob Walls <jacobtylerwalls@gmail.com>'
findings.recommendation: Excellent practice. Continue encouraging co-authorship attribution for collaborative work.

findings.description: Comprehensive governance documentation is present with CONTRIBUTING.rst providing clear guidelines and linking to extensive online documentation.
findings.evidence: CONTRIBUTING.rst (1,115 bytes) found in repository root. Content includes contribution examples, links to docs/internals/contributing/, online docs at https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/dev/internals/contributing/, clear warning about Trac requirement, and Code of Conduct reference.
findings.recommendation: Governance documentation is excellent. Maintain current standards.

findings.description: Code of Conduct is established and referenced, linking to the Django Foundation's organization-level conduct policy.
findings.evidence: .github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (70 bytes) present. CONTRIBUTING.rst states: 'As a contributor, you can help us keep the Django community open and inclusive. Please read and follow our Code of Conduct https://www.djangoproject.com/conduct/'
findings.recommendation: Excellent. The external CoC on djangoproject.com indicates foundation-level governance, which is appropriate for a project of this scale.

findings.description: Additional governance infrastructure includes security policy, PR template, funding information, and modern development tooling support.
findings.evidence: .github directory contains: SECURITY.md (80 bytes), pull_request_template.md (1,616 bytes), FUNDING.yml (68 bytes), and copilot-instructions.md (316 bytes)
findings.recommendation: Comprehensive governance infrastructure is in place. This represents best practices for mature open-source projects.

findings.description: Active and responsive maintainer engagement with rapid turnaround from contribution to merge. New contributors successfully getting patches merged.
findings.evidence: All 10 recent commits reference resolved Trac tickets (e.g., 'Fixed #37057', 'Fixed #35870', 'Fixed #36991', 'Fixed #15759'). Commits authored in March-April 2026 and committed in April 2026 show timely review cycles. New contributor DineshThumma9's commit dc467fdc ('Fixed #36991') merged April 22, 2026.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining current review velocity and welcoming approach to new contributors.

findings.description: Multiple maintainers actively reviewing and merging contributions, preventing bottlenecks.
findings.evidence: Recent commits show multiple committers: jacobtylerwalls (6 merges), sarahboyce (1 merge), web-flow/GitHub automated merge (1). This indicates distributed review and merge authority among core maintainers.
findings.recommendation: Excellent distribution of merge authority. Continue empowering multiple maintainers to prevent single points of failure.

findings.description: Exceptional release documentation with comprehensive, well-maintained release notes for all versions and patches.
findings.evidence: search_code for 'repo:django/django path:docs/releases' returned total_count=248 release note files. Recent versions include 6.1.txt, 3.0.8.txt, 1.8.txt, 1.6.3.txt, 1.4.2.txt showing both major releases and patch releases are documented.
findings.recommendation: This is exemplary practice. Continue maintaining detailed release notes for every version.

findings.description: Active ongoing development with continuous improvements and regular commits to main branch.
findings.evidence: Most recent commit 61a62be3 dated April 22, 2026 at 21:28:35Z. All 10 recent commits are from April 2026 (spanning April 19-22), indicating daily active development. Commits reference both bug fixes and feature work.
findings.recommendation: Development velocity is excellent. Continue current release and development practices.

findings.description: Hybrid GitHub/Trac workflow creates potential onboarding friction for GitHub-native contributors unfamiliar with Trac systems.
findings.evidence: CONTRIBUTING.rst requires Trac tickets: 'Warning: non-trivial pull requests (anything more than fixing a typo) without Trac tickets will be closed!' GitHub Issues disabled (totalCount=0). This workflow is intentional but differs from standard GitHub-only workflows.
findings.recommendation: Consider creating supplementary onboarding materials (video walkthrough, illustrated guide) specifically for the Trac workflow to reduce friction for first-time contributors. The current text documentation is clear but could be enhanced with visual guides.

findings.description: Excellent README.rst with comprehensive project overview, installation, usage, and contribution guidance. The README is concise (2,017 bytes) yet covers all essential information: project description, documentation structure, installation path (docs/intro/install.txt), tutorial sequence (tutorial01-08), deployment guides, community links (Django Discord, Forum), contribution guidelines, testing instructions, and funding information.
findings.evidence: README.rst file confirmed at repository root (2,017 bytes, SHA: b7532e6b5ad352fe5d560962f8c59356d2424fae). Content includes: 'Django is a high-level Python web framework...', references to docs/intro/install.txt, docs/intro/tutorial01.txt through tutorial08.txt, docs/howto/deployment/index.txt, https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/stable/, https://chat.djangoproject.com, https://forum.djangoproject.com/, and https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/dev/internals/contributing/
findings.recommendation: Consider adding repository status badges (CI/CD status, documentation build status, coverage) to provide quick health indicators for potential contributors.

findings.description: Exceptional documentation infrastructure with comprehensive Sphinx-based documentation system. The docs/ directory contains extensive subdirectories for tutorials (docs/intro/), API reference (docs/ref/), topical guides (docs/topics/), how-to guides (docs/howto/), FAQ (docs/faq/), and internals documentation (docs/internals/). Source code demonstrates consistent use of docstrings for modules, functions, and classes.
findings.evidence: docs/ directory confirmed with multiple subdirectories including intro/, ref/, topics/, howto/, faq/, internals/, releases/. Sphinx infrastructure files: docs/conf.py (14,810 bytes), docs/Makefile (7,125 bytes), docs/make.bat (6,056 bytes), docs/README.rst (655 bytes). ReadTheDocs integration via .readthedocs.yml (410 bytes). Source code example django/shortcuts.py shows module docstring and function-level docstrings for render(), redirect(), get_object_or_404(), aget_object_or_404(), get_list_or_404(), aget_list_or_404(), resolve_url(). Documentation linting via docs/lint.py (7,095 bytes) and spell-checking support via docs/spelling_wordlist (5,665 bytes).
findings.recommendation: Documentation is exemplary. Consider adding type hints inline with code to complement existing docstrings for enhanced IDE support and static analysis.

findings.description: Comprehensive release documentation with structured release notes for all versions. The docs/releases/ directory contains individual release note files following a consistent naming convention (VERSION.txt). Release history spans from early versions (1.5.1) to current versions (5.1), demonstrating long-term maintenance and clear versioning.
findings.evidence: docs/releases/ directory confirmed in repository tree. Release files found include docs/releases/5.1.txt (SHA: 32d32cf9e769d408e6854ecf6794a16483a4ce22) and docs/releases/1.5.1.txt (SHA: e45a0d826034aed7df8d4f54299700fa5ab02091). Search query returned 2 release files with pattern indicating many more versions documented.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a root-level CHANGELOG.md following the Keep a Changelog format for improved discoverability by GitHub users, while maintaining the detailed docs/releases/ structure for comprehensive release documentation.

findings.description: Clear contributing guide present at repository root with important workflow clarifications. CONTRIBUTING.rst (1,115 bytes) explicitly states contribution types (code patches, documentation improvements, bug reports, patch reviews), references extensive guidelines in docs/internals/contributing/, explains the Trac + GitHub workflow, and includes Code of Conduct reference. Critical warning included about non-trivial PRs requiring Trac tickets.
findings.evidence: CONTRIBUTING.rst file confirmed (1,115 bytes, SHA: 4b2ab363660df5b4b43e8e263b70ddb96b1db54e). Content includes: examples of contributions, reference to docs/internals/contributing/ and https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/dev/internals/contributing/, warning about PR requirements, link to https://code.djangoproject.com/newticket, explanation of Trac usage, and Code of Conduct link to https://www.djangoproject.com/conduct/. AUTHORS file present (45,163 bytes) crediting contributors.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a quick-start development setup section directly in CONTRIBUTING.rst (e.g., virtualenv setup, dependency installation) for immediate visibility, while maintaining detailed documentation in docs/internals/contributing/.

findings.description: Exceptional tutorial and example documentation with comprehensive learning path. The repository contains an 8-part tutorial series (docs/intro/tutorial01.txt through tutorial08.txt) plus extensive how-to guides, topical guides, FAQ section, and glossary. Documentation follows best practices by separating learning-oriented tutorials from task-oriented how-tos and reference material.
findings.evidence: docs/intro/ directory confirmed containing tutorial files. Search for 'intro tutorial' returned 40 total results including: docs/intro/tutorial08.txt, docs/intro/tutorial01.txt, docs/intro/tutorial03.txt, docs/intro/tutorial04.txt, docs/intro/tutorial07.txt. Additional directories confirmed: docs/howto/ (how-to guides), docs/topics/ (topical guides), docs/faq/ (FAQ), docs/glossary.txt (2,329 bytes), docs/index.txt (12,671 bytes main index). README.rst references installation guide at docs/intro/install.txt and deployment at docs/howto/deployment/index.txt.
findings.recommendation: Tutorial coverage is excellent. Consider adding a dedicated examples/ directory in the repository root with standalone code samples that users can clone and run independently of the main framework.

findings.description: Professional documentation tooling and quality assurance infrastructure. The repository includes documentation linting, spell-checking, multi-platform build support (Linux/Windows), ReadTheDocs integration, custom Sphinx theme, and man pages for command-line tools.
findings.evidence: Documentation infrastructure files confirmed: docs/lint.py (7,095 bytes for linting), docs/spelling_wordlist (5,665 bytes for spell-checking), docs/Makefile (7,125 bytes for Unix), docs/make.bat (6,056 bytes for Windows), .readthedocs.yml (410 bytes for automated builds), docs/_theme/ directory (custom theme), docs/man/ directory (man pages), docs/requirements.txt (72 bytes for doc dependencies).
findings.recommendation: Documentation infrastructure is exemplary. No improvements needed in this area.
